channel.title: Flooding on The Tradewinds Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Flooding on The Tradewinds Brief

item.title: Aenon Town Cut Off as Clarendon Flooding Worsens, MP Calls for Emergency Drainage Audit
item.description: Residents stranded across multiple routes as Aenon Town floods for the second consecutive day. MP Wavel Hinds is pressing for a full drainage audit, and the backdrop is a post-Melissa infrastructure system that still hasn't recovered.

item.title: Daily Brief – Wednesday, April 1, 2026
item.description: Your Wednesday morning roundup of Guyana's top stories — flooding warnings, digital IDs, oil drama, and the AFC demanding government fork over $9 billion.

item.title: Daily Brief — Tuesday, March 31, 2026
item.description: Oil hits 918K bpd, Georgetown floods, GUYOIL raises fuel prices, a teen is stabbed in Lacytown, and Uncle Sam wants his tax treaty. Your Tuesday briefing.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: De Brief Conveniently Forget Who Actually Building This Country
item.description: Uncle Ramesh has some thoughts about handrails, fuel prices, and why Guyana getting a field hospital in Essequibo is actually a big deal.

item.title: Monday Brief: Guyana Lectures the Caribbean on Climate, Then Drowns
item.description: Guyana told the Caribbean to climate-proof their infrastructure, then spent the weekend underwater. Also: Guyoil raised gas prices, the AFC says the 50% profit share is a fantasy, and we got the handrails on the US$6.8B FPSO.

item.title: De Boys Seh: Whose Streets Are These Exactly?
item.description: De boys at de corner discuss de government taking over Georgetown streets, de flooding, why de powerships still here, and Rihanna makeup landing in Guyana.

item.title: Speedeet & Wilar: De Day De Rain Come Down
item.description: Georgetown flooding again, and Speedeet see it as an opportunity. Wilar see it as a problem. Dem both right.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: De Brief Forget To Mention All De GOOD Tings Happening!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads de Sunday Brief and has some thoughts about what dem left out. As usual.

item.title: Sunday Brief: Georgetown Flood, Karpowership Extension & The Streets That Used To Be Ours
item.description: Georgetown flooded again, the powerships are staying longer than expected, the government and the Mayor are fighting over who owns the streets, and Rihanna's makeup is now available in Guyana. Sunday, bai.

item.title: ☕ The Daily Brief – Friday, February 13, 2026
item.description: Stabroek News announces closure after 39 years. Georgetown swamped by 4-inch rainfall. Mohamed’s cambio evidence mounts. Mottley wins AGAIN. Oil boom stealing police officers. And rockets are launching from our backyard.
